articles.title: Takaichi faces heat over defamatory campaign video scandal
articles.summary: A report claims that her campaign posted defamatory videos of her rivals on social media during election time. The release of audio recordings has intensified the controversy.

articles.title: Key suspect in Tochigi murder-robbery put on Interpol wanted list
articles.summary: At the request of Japanese police, the International Criminal Police Organization has issued a Red Notice for Kazuhiko Masuda, 48.

articles.title: Japan steps up efforts on cooking oil in race for sustainable aviation fuel​​
articles.summary: The world's fourth-biggest economy estimates it needs about 1.7 million kiloliters in 2030, and hopes to gain as much as it can domestically through used cooking oil.

articles.title: Asuka-Fujiwara ancient capitals to join World Heritage list
articles.summary: A UNESCO advisory panel has recommended adding the archaeological sites in western Japan to the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage list.

articles.title: Ex-residents to hold memorial services near Japan-claimed isles
articles.summary: Former residents of Russian-held northwestern Pacific islands claimed by Japan will hold memorial services for their ancestors at sea and in the air between July 25 and Nov. 7, the prefectural government of Hokkaido said Friday.

articles.title: Yen falls to pre-intervention levels after solid U.S. jobs data
articles.summary: As of 5 p.m. Friday, the greenback stood at ¥160.28 to 160.38, up ¥0.31 from the previous day.

articles.title: AI saves time but most companies waste the gain, study shows
articles.summary: The findings belie the premise that companies will automatically boost productivity through AI.

articles.title: On patrol with Tokyo’s new litter police
articles.summary: On the streets of Shibuya, patrol teams caught 10 people littering on the first day of the new ordinance, 15 on the second day and nine on the third, according to city officials.

articles.title: Japan and Indonesia to begin talks on exporting Asagiri-class destroyers
articles.summary: The agreement to begin working-level discussions is the latest in a push by Tokyo to transfer defense equipment to like-minded partners.

articles.title: Study warns biodiversity loss could trigger wave of debt crises
articles.summary: The study presented what they described as the world's first biodiversity-adjusted sovereign credit ratings model.

articles.title: Trump’s latest tariff salvo no fix for global issue of forced labor
articles.summary: Experts, business groups and some human rights groups say the move could actually make things worse.

articles.title: Trump’s ’crazy’ rebuke undercuts Netanyahu at a critical moment
articles.summary: A leaked phone call this week laid bare the strains that have at times emerged between the two leaders.

articles.title: U.S. attacks Iranian coastal sites after Iran launches drones in latest flare-up
articles.summary: U.S. forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: In Japan, a bull market for bear deterrents
articles.summary: With no end in sight to the rise in bear encounters, manufacturers are bringing new kinds of deterrents to the market.

articles.title: At 1 Hotel Tokyo, luxury is a zero-waste journey
articles.summary: Open from March, the brand's first outpost in Japan stays true to its sustainable roots.

articles.title: ‘Never After Dark’ finds fresh chills in familiar J-horror territory
articles.summary: Written and directed by Dave Boyle, the film begins as a paranormal investigation and transforms into another type of horror story altogether.

articles.title: ‘Fujiko’ is a stirring portrayal of a scrappy single mom
articles.summary: Yuki Katayama shines as the titular character in "Fujiko," a film about a woman's quest for autonomy in the 1970s and '80s.

articles.title: Parliament leaders adopt draft proposal for imperial succession
articles.summary: The draft calls for allowing female members of the imperial family to retain their status after marriage and male heirs to be adopted from former branches.

articles.title: Congo’s traditional healers are on the front line of Ebola fight
articles.summary: In a region where health workers are shunned, an effective response to Ebola relies on integrating communities and "their rites," the World Health Organization says.

articles.title: Man arrested over ¥15 million December burglary in western Tokyo
articles.summary: The man admitted to allegations of being involved in burglary in December, telling investigators he recruited the offenders and gave instructions.

articles.title: Mo Johnson relishing golden World Cup adventure
articles.summary: Moesha Johnson is enjoying an overseas open water swimming adventure that has seen her win gold at every stage. But these are all just stepping stones for the biggest prize of all at the Los Angeles Olympics in 2028.

articles.title: Trump battles historians for ownership of US presidential records
articles.summary: The Trump administration is pushing for a return to pre-Nixon days when US presidents could do whatever they wanted with presidential records.

articles.title: India's 'cockroach' political movement spills onto Delhi's streets
articles.summary: India's largest online youth movement has taken to the streets for the first time, calling for the resignation of the education minister.

articles.title: 'We want trophies': The Diamonds coach embracing pressure in pursuit of history
articles.summary: The world has never been closer to Australia's Diamonds. Coach Stacey Marinkovich is determined to keep them shining brightest.

articles.title: Single, lost and desperate, Harsimran turned to an 'employment mentor'
articles.summary: Personalised support helped domestic violence survivor and single mother Harsimran get a job, despite significant barriers. It's an approach that could benefit more jobseekers following the federal government's revamp of Workforce Australia.

articles.title: 'Rain bomb', mini tornadoes: Debunking Australia's greatest weather myths
articles.summary: Meteorologic rhetoric in Australia, including from the media, can at times be inaccurate, misconstrued or just completely fictitious.

articles.title: How looksmaxxing trend is affecting young men
articles.summary: While it's a bit of fun for some, the social media trend known as looksmaxxing can have major impacts on young men's physical and mental health.

articles.title: Will Australia be taken for a ride in the AI and data centre boom?
articles.summary: More than $150 billion is lined up for data centre development and investment in Australia, but there are questions as to whether the boom will provide benefits beyond a short-term construction boost.

articles.title: As vaccination rates fall in Australia, a British mother has a warning
articles.summary: Measles killed Rebecca Archer's daughter Renae slowly and silently over a decade. She has a message for the growing number of parents hesitant to have their children vaccinated.

articles.title: Get a buzz out of spelling? It's time to show off your skills
articles.summary: Admit it, you can't get enough of my synonyms and similes. Let's get spelling!

articles.title: Planning, packing and nerves: Six Aussies kick off Race Around The World
articles.summary: Six Australian filmmakers are about to embark on the journey of a lifetime on Race Around The World. Here's how they're preparing for the unknown.

articles.title: At Melbourne's last video rental store, physical media never died
articles.summary: CDs, DVDs, Blue-Rays and even VHS cassettes are enjoying a resurgence. Why some are returning to physical media?

articles.title: Live: Switzerland takes early lead as Volpato starts on Socceroos debut
articles.summary: Australia's Socceroos take on Switzerland in San Diego in their final warm-up match before the FIFA World Cup gets underway. Follow live.

articles.title: Russian oil facilities hit by second Ukrainian drone attack after Putin rejects talks
articles.summary: One person is dead and an oil depot has been set ablaze after a series of drone attacks waged by Ukraine on the Russian city of St Petersburg.

articles.title: Andrew Moloney claims world title two years after quitting in anger
articles.summary: Andrew Moloney weathered champion Willibaldo Garcia's best over 12 brutal rounds to win a majority decision in their super flyweight world title clash in Japan.

articles.title: 'We were so close': Laos cave rescue called off as conditions deteriorate
articles.summary: The search for two men trapped in a flooded cave in Laos has been postponed as rescuers say a "miracle" is needed.

articles.title: Shots fired where gangster's funeral was advertised to be held
articles.summary: The ABC understands the funeral of Sydney gangster Lorenzo Lemalu was originally scheduled to take place at the venue in Punchbowl, but the plans were changed.

articles.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas
articles.summary: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

articles.title: Broncos' season in crisis as Titans young gun conjures golden moment
articles.summary: Keano Kini scores one of the great game-winning tries to condemn Brisbane to a fifth-straight defeat, with the Titans snatching a 28-23 victory at Lang Park.

articles.title: Volpato admits he was 'a bit too scared' to make switch from Italy
articles.summary: New Socceroo Cristian Volpato opens up on his decision to switch allegiance from Italy to Australia ahead of the FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: BYD plans to install its Flash Chargers at Sinopec petrol stations
articles.summary: BYD has formed a partnership in China with Sinopec to accelerate the expansion of its “Flash Charging” fast-charging network in the country. The move leverages Sinopec’s nationwide footprint of more than 30,000 petrol stations.

articles.title: First drive in the MG4 EV Urban: Not exciting – but surprisingly good
articles.summary: The new MG4 EV Urban makes its mission clear from the name: everyday usability, commuting and straightforward electric mobility. Instead of relying on large displays, exaggerated sportiness or flashy design, MG Motor takes a pragmatic approach. A first drive through urban traffic, along country roads and on the motorway towards Lake Starnberg reveals whether that concept works in practice.

articles.title: Samsung SDI reportedly to supply battery cells to Volkswagen
articles.summary: Volkswagen is reportedly bringing the South Korean battery manufacturer Samsung SDI on board as an additional supplier for its Unified Cell. The standardised battery cell is currently produced by VW subsidiary PowerCo in Salzgitter and by VW affiliate Gotion in China.

articles.title: UK EV market share reaches 27.3% in strongest May since 2019
articles.summary: Battery-electric vehicle registrations in the UK continued to accelerate in May, with BEV volumes rising by 34.2% year-on-year to achieve a market share of 27.3%. According to new figures published by the Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders (SMMT), the result marks the highest monthly BEV share recorded so far in 2026.

articles.title: Rivian Adventure Network announces fast charging milestone
articles.summary: Californian EV startup Rivian has announced that its fast charging network now has more than 1000 DC fast charging ports located across the USA. Four sites featuring a total of 44 charging stalls were opened in the last month alone.

articles.title: EZO secures £176m contract in England’s Midlands
articles.summary: UK charging provider EZO has secured a £176 million contract to deploy and operate EV charging infrastructure across four Midlands councils. The 15-year project will add 250 rapid and ultra-rapid chargers and support more than two million residents.

articles.title: i-charging unveils 500 kW charging system for cars and trucks
articles.summary: Portuguese charging infrastructure manufacturer i-charging has introduced its new All-in-One 500 (AiO 500) charging system with up to 500 kW of charging power. The station supports CCS, NACS and GB/T standards, covering the most widely used international charging formats. According to the company, the optional liquid-cooled charging cables also enable regular charging for electric trucks.
